clinical_information.prior_treatments_and_results: Current steroid therapy with Methylprednisone 40mg daily; patient continues to have frequent blood in stools and anemia as well as abdominal discomfort.
clinical_information.lab_summary: The lab results indicate significant abnormalities including low hemoglobin (9.0 g/dL), low hematocrit (32%), low red blood cells (3.5 million/uL), and low mean corpuscular volume (78 fL), suggesting anemia. Additionally, there is a high neutrophil percentage (75%) and elevated inflammatory markers with a high ESR (30 mm/h) and CRP (25 mg/L). Fecal markers show a high fecal calprotectin (150 ug/g) and positive fecal occult blood, indicating possible gastrointestinal inflammation or bleeding.

EGD.duodenum: Mild to moderate duodenitis with edema

colonoscopy.ileum: Patchy inflammation with areas of erythema and ulceration. Segmental strictures observed.
colonoscopy.colon: Diffuse inflammation with cobblestone appearance. Multiple aphthous ulcers noted throughout the colon with areas of normal mucosa between inflamed portions. Granularity and friability present.

clinical_information.symptom_severity_and_impact: Patient has abdominal pain, hematochezia, pallor, and dizziness, indicating significant symptom severity.
clinical_information.prognosis_and_risk_if_not_approved: Patient is symptomatic with anemia and may require transfusion; risk of worsening symptoms and complications if biologic therapy is not initiated.
clinical_information.clinical_rationale_for_urgency: Patient will likely need to initiate biologic therapy given severity of symptoms despite current therapy.

treatment_request.dosage: 40mg every other week starting 2 weeks from 2nd dose
treatment_request.rationale: Patient has evidence of new diagnosis of Crohn's disease, is currently hospitalized due to a flare and is not improving on standard therapy (methylprednisone).
